content: If we borrow the language learning definition of reflexion as both a core vocabulary concept and a metacognitive learning practice, we can frame LLM self-reflection mechanisms as a form of second-language acquisition for AI, where reflexion is the core vocabulary an LLM uses to describe and refine its own reasoning outputs. Just as high school learners master reflexion as both a word and a thinking practice to improve their writing, LLMs can build dynamic self-reflection memory by explicitly treating reflexion vocabulary as a framework to anchor inconsistent solving perspectives.
